reviews.review_msg: Daniel was the toughest grader I've encountered at sfu, and he left little feedback. I scored an 85 on an assignment, but it only had one note about a better word I could've used. I don't know where the rest of my grade went? This class is hard and boring, BUT it made me a much better writer. I feel like I understand academic writing now.

Daniel is a great Prof. He has clear instructions for his assignments and is reasonable when asking for help. He actually supports your learning and makes the class fun. The small class size is also nice. You do need to work hard on your assignments tho. You also have to pay attention in class or you won't understand the assignments.

Daniel taught me a lot about rhetoric writing, and the small class size makes him more approachable during office hours. Be sure to read the criteria carefully when writing assignments because there are essential components that you must put in. Overall, it was a fun class with only writing!

English is really not my forte and not having clear criteria laid out makes this class super difficult for me. However, Daniel is an excellent lecturer and I learned a lot about academic writing from this class.
Hes a great professor and gives really good feedback. His criteria isnt that specific but hes not a hard marker. This class really helped me develop my writing,

I love his teaching style. He offers so many extra interesting ideas/pieces of literature that not only help with your writing but your critical thinking. A lot of people put stress on the fact that his instructions are not direct but that is the point; he is trying to give us freedom in what we write. To me, this was great and I enjoyed writing.

He's a fun professor but he's also very vague about what the criteria are for the assignments so there's a lot of questions and he has a tendency to answer questions with, "you should know that by now." Yeah, that definitely wasn't embarrassing :/ He's pretty chill and gives good feedback tho.

I do not understand the good reviews for Professor Dunford. He has no outlines for his assignments which means you have to scramble to figure out how to do them, and he is extremely cold when you ask for help. You cannot even easily figure out how many pages your assignments have to be without jumping through hoops. So unnecessarily difficult.
Great professor. Super nice guy who clearly has an interest in the subject. Just wished he explained grading/gave a rubric for the essays. Awesome prof nonetheless.

English 199 sparked an interest in English rhetoric for me. After having taken his class, not only did I feel like I learned something about writing papers, but I also had fun doing so. That being said, attendance is mandatory, and there is quite a workload. If you keep up with your weekly assignments and engage in class discussion, you'll pass!

Daniel is a great professor. I've only taken Engl 199 with him, but the course was really helpful as an intro into university writing and such. He's a fair marker and actually gives very good feedback during office hours which I highly recommend you visit. I'm not even an English major and still managed to get a great mark .
Hard but fun class. So much work you wont believe it but the actual seminars were fun to me.

Daniel Dunford is one of the best English professors at SFU. He is genuinely interested in the material and his lectures are never boring. In addition, his marking is fair and gives tons of feedback on assignments. Would definitely consider taking a course with him again in the future if I have the chance.

His experience with the subject and the fact that he also teaches EDUC courses makes for a great class. His enthusiasm for the subject can be infectious and, at times, intimidating. While he can be intimidating, if you approach him with surety, youll have no problems, even if the all youre sure about is that youre unsure.
Very average prof. Tons of assignments and grading criteria is not very clear. Seminars were interesting and engaging. Not a superbly kind or approachable guy, but also not mean.

Don't take this course with Daniel. He is a nice person but not a nice prof. Only if he likes you, you will get a good grade, otherwise, you will only get passing grade. I'm regret to see the comment on the rate my prof, which lead me to choose this prof.

Daniel Dunford is pretty hilarious, although at times he can be intimidating. Plenty of homework but not necessary as the class goes over it together as a group. His passion for teaching is shown through his thoughts and actions.
good prof.
He is amazing,very passionate about the material. Always there to help and tells interesting stories. It is a pleasure listening to him.

LOVED THIS PROF! He is the best prof I have had so far. He was very nice and approachable. Very funny as well. Aside from teaching you FALX99 he teaches you life lessons! Very helpful for Academic Writing. I wish I could get another class with him.

Daniel Dunford was more than an instructor to me. He was inspirational and life-changing. Simply put, he changed the whole way I look at the English language and  breathed new life into my writing, speaking, and thinking. Due to the grace of God and what this man taught me, I was able to graduate with a cumulative G.P.A. above 3.5. Thank you sir!
This is a useful class as long as you are going to continue university studies and other professions related to what you learn in this class.
No comment for him!
Never let people explain against his thought!
A lot of works,but nice teacher.

Cheerful guy. ALOT of work. really ask him what he wants before you start doing your big assignments. attendance is mandatory since its a 17 person class. material is not hard..but different and pretty technical. really improves the way you write research institute papers/essays.
Was a nice enough guy, but the gives way too much work.

Super fellow and a great professor.  Keeps you jumping a bit but ties it all together.  Fair marker and spends a lot of time to provide useful criticism to your writing.  Very witty and keeps things current.  Excellent class.  Learned loads.
be prepared for lots and lots of completly useless writing..not the kinda of intro to writing class youd expect..okay prof but material is not so great
Dunford is an awesome professor. Very easy-going. He manages to make his English class interesting and enjoyable.
easy course.he's nice and easy-going! although exercises in writing aren't very helpful but this course is def. a GPA booster! i'll take his 199 class for sure next sem.

Excellent Teacher, Funny, Cynical, extremely interesting. He really excels at making the class feel good when they are participating. He is a teacher I will remember 20 years down the line. He even helped me like Aristotle or should I say persuaded me to like Aristotle?
lots of work for portfolio, but really taght me how to write in academic genre. very nice guy, I'll take his course again! stronly recommended.
very nice guy, fair marker and funny. really enjoyed this course.
Other students will say that he gives too much work. But a course cannot be good without being demanding. There are too few like him...
helpful, makes class interesting
An excellent teacher.  Funny, informative, knowledgeable with a clear interest in the subject.  Would recommend him to anyone interested in the subject
Stresses clarity on assignments, but is somewhat vague if you ask him questions regarding assignments.  Fair at marking, but lots of work in his class!
Organized and direct. Teaches well but the workload impressed upon the students is quite stressful.
Made the class fun... an ok teacher
